{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "5a297b03",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "(modules-kwn-precipitation-316h)=\n",
    "# 316H two-phase precipitation (carbide + Laves)\n",
    "\n",
    "This worked example assembles a two-phase precipitation model for 316H\n",
    "austenitic stainless steel from the [](modules-kwn) catalog and runs it across a\n",
    "range of service temperatures. Two precipitate populations compete for the same\n",
    "matrix solute reservoir:\n",
    "\n",
    "- $\\mathrm{Cr}_{23}\\mathrm{C}_6$ carbide — rate-limited by chromium,\n",
    "- $\\mathrm{Fe}_2\\mathrm{Mo}$ Laves phase — rate-limited by molybdenum.\n",
    "\n",
    "Rather than tracking a single mean radius per phase, we solve the full\n",
    "population-balance equation for each phase's size distribution $n(R,t)$ on a\n",
    "shared radius grid, so nucleation, growth, and coarsening all emerge from the\n",
    "same upwinded advection in radius space.\n",
    "\n",
    "The material data and composition follow\n",
    "\n",
    "> J. Hu, G. Green, S. Hogg, R. Higginson, A. Cocks, *Effect of microstructure\n",
    "> evolution on the creep properties of a polycrystalline 316H austenitic\n",
    "> stainless steel*, Materials Science & Engineering A 772 (2020) 138787,\n",
    "> [doi:10.1016/j.msea.2019.138787](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.msea.2019.138787).\n",
    "\n",
    "See [](modules-kwn) for the governing equations and the catalog of primitives\n",
    "wired together below."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "ad3950b4",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Material data\n",
    "\n",
    "All values live in `precipitation_316h.i` (next to this notebook); they are\n",
    "reproduced here so the example is self-documenting. Units throughout:\n",
    "length = μm, time = hr, concentration = mass fraction.\n",
    "\n",
    "| Quantity | Carbide ($\\mathrm{Cr}_{23}\\mathrm{C}_6$) | Laves ($\\mathrm{Fe}_2\\mathrm{Mo}$) |\n",
    "| --- | ---: | ---: |\n",
    "| Surface energy $\\gamma$ (J/m²) | 0.30 | 0.25 |\n",
    "| Molar volume $V_m$ (m³/mol) | 6×10⁻⁶ | 2×10⁻⁵ |\n",
    "| Nucleation site density (1/m³) | 1×10¹⁵ | 5×10¹⁴ |\n",
    "| Rate-limiting species | Cr | Mo |\n",
    "\n",
    "| Diffusion | $D_0$ (m²/s) | $Q$ (kJ/mol) |\n",
    "| --- | ---: | ---: |\n",
    "| Cr | 1.5×10⁻⁴ | 240 |\n",
    "| Mo | 7.4×10⁻⁴ | 283 |\n",
    "\n",
    "Initial matrix mass fractions (after deducting 0.8 vol% pre-existing\n",
    "intergranular carbide): $x_{0,\\mathrm{Cr}} = 0.1625$,\n",
    "$x_{0,\\mathrm{C}} = 3.75\\times10^{-4}$, $x_{0,\\mathrm{Mo}} = 0.0233$. The\n",
    "equilibrium matrix and precipitate compositions at the tabulated temperatures\n",
    "come from the ThermoCalc values reported in the paper and are interpolated with\n",
    "`ScalarLinearInterpolation`."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "f77efbaf",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Set up\n",
    "\n",
    "`precipitation_316h.i` composes the KWN primitives (volume fraction, mass-balance\n",
    "concentration, projected diffusivity, ideal-solution driving force, the\n",
    "classical-nucleation chain, and the rate-limited growth rate) with the\n",
    "[](modules-finite-volume) advection machinery, once per phase, and wraps the\n",
    "population balance in an `ImplicitUpdate` Newton solve. The model evaluates on\n",
    "the GPU when one is available; we work in double precision throughout."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "e2ee3ea4",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-28T23:54:54.156884Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-28T23:54:54.156042Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-28T23:54:57.179268Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-28T23:54:57.176882Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Running on cuda:0\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import torch\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "import neml2\n",
    "from neml2.types import Scalar\n",
    "from neml2.aoti import ConvergenceError\n",
    "\n",
    "torch.set_default_dtype(torch.double)\n",
    "device = torch.device(\"cuda:0\" if torch.cuda.is_available() else \"cpu\")\n",
    "print(f\"Running on {device}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "7d923069",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Load the model and build the radius grid\n",
    "\n",
    "Both phases share one logarithmic radius grid spanning 0.1 nm to 1 μm (200\n",
    "cells). The grid is built inside the input file; we mirror the cell centers here\n",
    "for plotting."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "32b2a3cc",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-28T23:54:57.184878Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-28T23:54:57.184049Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-28T23:54:57.345490Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-28T23:54:57.343881Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "ComposedModel(\n",
       "  (model_step): ImplicitUpdate(\n",
       "    (implicit_rate): ComposedModel(\n",
       "      (D_Cr): ArrheniusParameter()\n",
       "      (D_Mo): ArrheniusParameter()\n",
       "      (Cr_eq): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "      (C_eq): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "      (Mo_eq): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "      (Cr_p_C): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "      (C_p_C): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "      (Mo_p_L): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "      (dx_C_Cr): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "      (dx_L_Mo): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "      (vol_fraction_C): PrecipitateVolumeFraction()\n",
       "      (vol_fraction_L): PrecipitateVolumeFraction()\n",
       "      (x_Cr): CurrentConcentration()\n",
       "      (x_C): CurrentConcentration()\n",
       "      (x_Mo): CurrentConcentration()\n",
       "      (S_C): ProjectedDiffusivitySum()\n",
       "      (growth_C): RateLimitedPrecipitateGrowthRate()\n",
       "      (dg_v_C): IdealSolutionVolumetricDrivingForce()\n",
       "      (S_L): ProjectedDiffusivitySum()\n",
       "      (dg_v_L): IdealSolutionVolumetricDrivingForce()\n",
       "      (growth_L): RateLimitedPrecipitateGrowthRate()\n",
       "      (v_edge_C): LinearlyInterpolateToCellEdges()\n",
       "      (barrier_C): NucleationBarrierAndCriticalRadius()\n",
       "      (barrier_L): NucleationBarrierAndCriticalRadius()\n",
       "      (v_edge_L): LinearlyInterpolateToCellEdges()\n",
       "      (flux_C): FiniteVolumeUpwindedAdvectiveFlux()\n",
       "      (Z_C): ZeldovichFactor()\n",
       "      (beta_C): KineticFactor()\n",
       "      (Z_L): ZeldovichFactor()\n",
       "      (beta_L): KineticFactor()\n",
       "      (flux_L): FiniteVolumeUpwindedAdvectiveFlux()\n",
       "      (flux_C_left): FiniteVolumeAppendBoundaryCondition()\n",
       "      (J_C): NucleationFluxMagnitude()\n",
       "      (J_L): NucleationFluxMagnitude()\n",
       "      (flux_L_left): FiniteVolumeAppendBoundaryCondition()\n",
       "      (flux_C_right): FiniteVolumeAppendBoundaryCondition()\n",
       "      (nuc_flux_C): DumpInSmallestBin()\n",
       "      (nuc_flux_L): DumpInSmallestBin()\n",
       "      (flux_L_right): FiniteVolumeAppendBoundaryCondition()\n",
       "      (flux_div_C): FiniteVolumeGradient()\n",
       "      (flux_div_L): FiniteVolumeGradient()\n",
       "      (n_dot_C): ScalarLinearCombination()\n",
       "      (n_dot_L): ScalarLinearCombination()\n",
       "      (integrate_C): ScalarBackwardEulerTimeIntegration()\n",
       "      (integrate_L): ScalarBackwardEulerTimeIntegration()\n",
       "    )\n",
       "    (predictor): ConstantExtrapolationPredictor()\n",
       "  )\n",
       "  (Cr_p_C): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "  (C_p_C): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "  (Mo_p_L): ScalarLinearInterpolation()\n",
       "  (vol_fraction_C): PrecipitateVolumeFraction()\n",
       "  (vol_fraction_L): PrecipitateVolumeFraction()\n",
       "  (x_Cr): CurrentConcentration()\n",
       "  (x_C): CurrentConcentration()\n",
       "  (x_Mo): CurrentConcentration()\n",
       ")"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 2,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "nbins = 200\n",
    "edges = torch.logspace(-4, 0, nbins + 1)  # 0.1 nm to 1 μm\n",
    "centers = 0.5 * (edges[:-1] + edges[1:])\n",
    "R = centers.numpy()\n",
    "\n",
    "model = neml2.load_model(\"precipitation_316h.i\", \"model\").to(device)\n",
    "out_names = list(model.output_spec.keys())\n",
    "model"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "e74acc58",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Time history\n",
    "\n",
    "We sweep four service temperatures (500–650 °C) over roughly four decades of\n",
    "time, stepping the implicit update from one log-spaced time point to the next.\n",
    "The temperatures form a plain batch axis, so all four histories advance in a\n",
    "single vectorized call per step.\n",
    "\n",
    "The carbide saturates first — and fastest at the highest temperature, which is\n",
    "also where the backward-Euler / Newton step becomes stiffest. If a step fails to\n",
    "converge we stop the sweep gracefully and keep the history collected so far\n",
    "(`ConvergenceError` is NEML2's recoverable solver-divergence signal)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "bfe0c6ce",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-28T23:54:57.350072Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-28T23:54:57.349924Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-28T23:55:05.417817Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-28T23:55:05.415743Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Stopped at step 63, t = 3.511e+01 hr (Newton did not converge).\n",
      "Completed 62 of 99 steps.\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "T_K = torch.tensor([773.15, 823.15, 873.15, 923.15], device=device)  # 500..650 °C\n",
    "times = torch.logspace(-1, 3, 100, device=device)  # 0.1 .. 1000 hr\n",
    "\n",
    "T = Scalar(T_K)\n",
    "ic = Scalar(torch.full((nbins,), 1e-15, device=device), sub_batch_ndim=1)  # tiny seed\n",
    "\n",
    "ndC = ndL = ic\n",
    "keys = (\"vf_C\", \"vf_L\", \"x_Cr\", \"x_C\", \"x_Mo\", \"number_density_C\", \"number_density_L\")\n",
    "trace = {k: [] for k in keys}\n",
    "t_done = []\n",
    "for i in range(1, len(times)):\n",
    "    # input_spec order: T, number_density_C~1, t, t~1, number_density_L~1\n",
    "    try:\n",
    "        outs = model(T, ndC, Scalar(times[i].clone()), Scalar(times[i - 1].clone()), ndL)\n",
    "    except ConvergenceError:\n",
    "        print(f\"Stopped at step {i}, t = {float(times[i]):.3e} hr (Newton did not converge).\")\n",
    "        break\n",
    "    res = dict(zip(out_names, outs))\n",
    "    ndC, ndL = res[\"number_density_C\"], res[\"number_density_L\"]\n",
    "    for k in keys:\n",
    "        trace[k].append(res[k].data.detach().cpu())\n",
    "    t_done.append(float(times[i]))\n",
    "\n",
    "t_done = np.array(t_done)\n",
    "trace = {k: torch.stack(v).numpy() for k, v in trace.items()}\n",
    "labels = [f\"{float(t) - 273.15:.0f} °C\" for t in T_K]\n",
    "print(f\"Completed {len(t_done)} of {len(times) - 1} steps.\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "31c8d4f2",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Volume fractions\n",
    "\n",
    "The carbide reaches its (small) saturation level far faster than Laves, and both\n",
    "accelerate with temperature."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "cfd4ac17",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-28T23:55:05.424049Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-28T23:55:05.423446Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-28T23:55:05.913184Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-28T23:55:05.911414Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x400 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4), sharex=True, sharey=True)\n",
    "for j, lbl in enumerate(labels):\n",
    "    axes[0].loglog(t_done, trace[\"vf_C\"][:, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "    axes[1].loglog(t_done, trace[\"vf_L\"][:, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "axes[0].set_title(\"Carbide  $\\\\mathrm{Cr}_{23}\\\\mathrm{C}_6$\")\n",
    "axes[1].set_title(\"Laves  $\\\\mathrm{Fe}_2\\\\mathrm{Mo}$\")\n",
    "for ax in axes:\n",
    "    ax.set_xlabel(\"Time (hr)\")\n",
    "    ax.set_ylabel(\"Volume fraction\")\n",
    "    ax.legend()\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8451e777",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Matrix solute concentrations\n",
    "\n",
    "Carbon is the limiting reagent for the carbide, so it depletes sharply; chromium\n",
    "is in large excess and barely moves; molybdenum is nearly flat because Laves\n",
    "nucleation is still in its lag phase over this window."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "15d082b2",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-28T23:55:05.916674Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-28T23:55:05.916536Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-28T23:55:06.411232Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-28T23:55:06.410072Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1300x400 with 3 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 4))\n",
    "for j, lbl in enumerate(labels):\n",
    "    axes[0].semilogx(t_done, trace[\"x_Cr\"][:, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "    axes[1].loglog(t_done, trace[\"x_C\"][:, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "    axes[2].semilogx(t_done, trace[\"x_Mo\"][:, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "axes[0].set_ylabel(\"$x_{\\\\mathrm{Cr}}$\")\n",
    "axes[1].set_ylabel(\"$x_{\\\\mathrm{C}}$\")\n",
    "axes[2].set_ylabel(\"$x_{\\\\mathrm{Mo}}$\")\n",
    "for ax in axes:\n",
    "    ax.set_xlabel(\"Time (hr)\")\n",
    "    ax.legend()\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "fae65caf",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Mean radius and number density\n",
    "\n",
    "The mean radius is the first moment of the size distribution,\n",
    "$\\bar R = \\sum_i R_i n_i / \\sum_i n_i$. Carbide coarsening shows up as a rising\n",
    "mean radius once nucleation has tailed off."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "0b4d385c",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-28T23:55:06.415705Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-28T23:55:06.415538Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-28T23:55:07.358150Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-28T23:55:07.356958Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x700 with 4 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def moments(n):\n",
    "    total = n.sum(axis=-1)\n",
    "    mean_R = (n * R).sum(axis=-1) / np.where(total > 0, total, 1.0)\n",
    "    return mean_R, total\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "mean_R_C, N_C = moments(trace[\"number_density_C\"])\n",
    "mean_R_L, N_L = moments(trace[\"number_density_L\"])\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(10, 7), sharex=True)\n",
    "for j, lbl in enumerate(labels):\n",
    "    axes[0, 0].loglog(t_done, mean_R_C[:, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "    axes[0, 1].loglog(t_done, mean_R_L[:, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "    axes[1, 0].loglog(t_done, N_C[:, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "    axes[1, 1].loglog(t_done, N_L[:, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "axes[0, 0].set_title(\"Carbide mean radius (μm)\")\n",
    "axes[0, 1].set_title(\"Laves mean radius (μm)\")\n",
    "axes[1, 0].set_title(\"Carbide number density (1/μm³)\")\n",
    "axes[1, 1].set_title(\"Laves number density (1/μm³)\")\n",
    "for ax in axes.flat:\n",
    "    ax.set_xlabel(\"Time (hr)\")\n",
    "    ax.legend()\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "805ff6ed",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Final size distributions\n",
    "\n",
    "A snapshot of $n(R)$ at the last converged step. The carbide distribution at the\n",
    "higher temperatures has spread toward larger radii by coarsening, while Laves is\n",
    "still close to its tiny seed."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",

   "id": "21b98ab5",

      ]

    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4), sharex=True)\n",
    "for j, lbl in enumerate(labels):\n",
    "    axes[0].loglog(R, trace[\"number_density_C\"][-1, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "    axes[1].loglog(R, trace[\"number_density_L\"][-1, j], label=lbl)\n",
    "axes[0].set_title(f\"Carbide $n(R)$ at $t = {t_done[-1]:.1e}$ hr\")\n",
    "axes[1].set_title(f\"Laves $n(R)$ at $t = {t_done[-1]:.1e}$ hr\")\n",
    "for ax in axes:\n",
    "    ax.set_xlabel(\"Radius (μm)\")\n",
    "    ax.set_ylabel(\"Number density (1/μm³)\")\n",
    "    ax.legend()\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()"
   ]
